column2: American paratroopers hold an orchard against a German counterattack. The road on board 9 from E1 to E9 is a dike road. All hexes on boards 6 and 2, and all hexes on board 9 on the dike road, are considered Orchards.
The American player wins if there are no German units on board 6, board 2, or on or west of the dike road on board 9 at the end of the game.

Company A (board 6, hex rows A-C): 3 Paratrooper Squads (full), 2 Paratrooper Squads (Reduced), 1 Paratrooper MG. Company C (board 9, on or west of the dike road): 6 Paratrooper Squads (full), 2 Paratrooper Squads (Reduced), 1 Paratrooper w/ Bazooka, 1 Paratrooper MG, 3 Paratrooper DECOY.
Board 2: 6 1st Line, 1 1st Line MG WT, 6 1st Line DECOY. Board 1: 9 1st Line, 1 1st Line MG WT, 1 1st Line DECOY.
The German player may exchange 1 1st Line MG WT and 1 1st Line DECOY to add 2 additional 1st Line Squads in that group.
